SOCIETY AND CULTURE
school system, however; is sound enough to make even an aver- age education good one_ Of the Successor States the Alliance rates hignest in tne quality the average individual s education_ The Federated Suns half of the Commonwealth has always had difficulty getting teachers funds and supplies to worlds that need them: Those fortunate enough to live on core worlds have countless choices and opportunities in the Outback; the traveling DropShip vagabond schools only game in town. The ruling House has too often faced the choice of feed: ing educating its  huge population. Thenation possesses some of the best higher-leve schools in the Inner Sphere but all are located New Avalon 0r one of the other core worlds Periphery nations feel the lack of education most bitterly: Perennially cash-strapped, they must work hard to provide even tair primary schools and these are rarely available to the entire population. Secondary schools and colleges are still rare the Periphery; though that is starting to change_ MONEY AND COMMERCE Money is essential to Inner Sphere ad Periphery soci- eties facilitating trade   of goods services and  information_ However, different currencies ad methods of use complicate transactions The following paragraphs offer brief run-down on local currencies, exchange rates and electronic funds, along with small section on the cost of cummun goods.
PRIVLEGES 0f War every Successor State and Periphery realm , one fact holds tue for education: more opportunities exist or those Inclined toward the military: Of course, even in the military; who you are can determine the opportunities You have The thlrd son of subsistence farmer on a Outback world has Iittle chance of becoming MechWarrior; he's Much more likely to end up a5 grunt infantry: In this as in all else , rank has its priv- ileges: Every Inner Sphere nation boasts at least one premier martial school: the NAIS College of Military Sciences, Tharkad $ Nagelring_ the Combine's Sun Zhang Academy; the Capellan Warrior Houses and the Allison MechWarrior Institute in the Free Worilds League. Graduates of such schools can claim pres- tige equal to the alumni of most purely academic colleges; the cream of thirty-first-century warriors are specialists with top- flight education: Even civilian universities have been touched by war: Many offer courses in military science , providing path t0 officer training: More often than not; research work aimed at redis - covering understanding the principles behind lost military technologies and  several  scholarships exist for those who agree to place Iheir services at the disposal of the government: Even in more politically open nations, citizens get clear mes - sage early in Ilife: service t0 the nation guarantees education:
LOCAL CURRENCIES AND EXCHANGE RATES The single currency used by the Inner Sphere during the time of the first Star League vanished after the Amaris coup and the Succession wars tnat  followcd. New national currencies arose in its place, generically called House bills (H-bills): today: these monies account for almost 90 percent of the currency used in the Inner Sphere_ The rest are ComStar bills (C-bills) planetary scrip (worthless anywhere except the planet on which was ssued).
In the Draconis Combine the upper crust receives stellar education perhaps the best the Inner Sphere. Most Combine citizens however, receive frce but ow-quality schooling: The Combine " heawy emphasis the needs of the state Over the student tends to stifle creativity and free thought: Students are typically barraged with aptitude tests with failures weeded out and funneled into technical schools The Capellan Confederation possesses one of the Inner Sphere Most comprehensive basic school systems, though as with the Combine the student" right to knowledge must give way to the needs of the state. All citizens are entitled to free pri- mary and secondary schooling; where necessary, the govern- ment often bears the expense of transporting children to worlds where these services are available and pays for their room and board_ The schools are often low quality; however; and the cur- riculum includes an inordinate amount of political indoctrina tion: The Confederation has very few higher-level schools_ small number of colleges and universities are the province nobility, warriors and those few from the lower castes who can prove their academic worth. The Free Worlds League and Lyran Alliance both boast fair systems of education that most people can afford. The League relies on nationwide standards t0 help ensure student compe- tence_ Secondary schooling_ however; IS extremely expensive  problem tne League has yet t0 solve. In the Lyran Alliance_ cost IS even more 0l an Issue _ with top quality education through the university level generally available only to the wealthy: The basic
Lyran Alllance The currency the Lyran Alliance and the Lyran Commonwealth before it, is the kroner (symbol: K or Sbill}: Each kroner is subdivided into 100 pfennigs_ Most major trans- actions are carried out electronically; but notes and coins remain common Coins exist in 1, and 10 pfennig denomina tions made of copper alloy; 25 and 50 pfennig_ made of silver alloy ; and and kroner; of Eold alloy: All coins are minted 0n Tharkad, with the Steiner emblem on one side and the Archon'$ image on the other: Paper notes exist for 5 10, 50, 100, 500, 1,000 and 10,000 kroner; all of which are color-coded and read: ily distinguishable. One side of each note bears the Archon'5 image and the Steiner insignia; while   the other depicts famous scene from Lyran history:
